description:
You recently started cleaning up a Python project that lives in the
    directory “/home/user/project”.  
    At the moment, the file structure looks like this:

        /home/user/project/
        └── src/
            └── main.py

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. Create a new sub-directory named “bin” inside “/home/user/project”.  
    2. Inside that “bin” directory, create a single symbolic link called
       “run_app” that points to “/home/user/project/src/main.py”.  
       • The link has to be relative, i.e. its link target must read “../src/main.py”.
    3. Produce a one-line text log so automated tests can verify your work.

    Required verification file  
    --------------------------  
    After finishing, create (or overwrite) the file

        /home/user/symlink-task/verification.log

    The file has to contain exactly one line (no leading or trailing blank
    lines, no extra spaces, no newline after the line).  
    That single line must be the **absolute canonical path** the link resolves
    to.  In other words, the content of the file must be identical to the
    output of the command:

        readlink -f /home/user/project/bin/run_app

    Given the required project layout, the line must therefore read:

        /home/user/project/src/main.py

    Summary checklist  
    -----------------  
    • “/home/user/project/bin/run_app” exists and is a symbolic link.  
    • The link itself is stored as “../src/main.py” (relative link).  
    • “/home/user/symlink-task/verification.log” exists and its *only* line
      is “/home/user/project/src/main.py”.
    • Nothing else needs to be printed, moved or modified.
truth:
Initial file tree that exists before the agent starts:

        /home/user/project/
        └── src/
            └── main.py

    The file “/home/user/project/src/main.py” already exists and contains:

        #!/usr/bin/env python3
        print("Hello from main!")

    The automated checker will verify the following after the agent finishes:

    1. Directory /home/user/project/bin exists (is a directory).
    2. Path /home/user/project/bin/run_app exists and is a symlink.
    3. Using os.readlink on that symlink returns exactly "../src/main.py".
    4. Resolving the symlink (readlink -f) returns
       "/home/user/project/src/main.py".
    5. File /home/user/symlink-task/verification.log exists and is readable.
    6. The entire content of /home/user/symlink-task/verification.log is exactly:

       /home/user/project/src/main.py

       (no newline after the string).
name: task_000000_648a1db3
